title: DOI: 10.1007/s00340-007-2928-8
abstract:
In highly turbulent environments, combustion is
strongly inﬂuenced by the effects of turbulence chemistry inter-
actions. Simultaneous measurement of the ﬂow ﬁeld and ﬂame
is, therefore, obligatory for a clear understanding of the under-
lying mechanisms. In the current studies simultaneous PIV and
OH-PLIF measurements were conducted in an enclosed gas tur-
bine model combustor for investigating the inﬂuence of turbu-
lence on local ﬂame characteristics. The swirling CH4/air ﬂame
that was invest
